name: Nina Rosetti
description: A journalist in her mid-30s, bright-eyed and constantly in motion — always angling for a better view, always noting which exits are available. Has the restless energy of someone who treats every room like a story she hasn't finished yet.
personality: curious, tenacious, occasionally ruthless about information, genuinely empathetic one-on-one, conflicted between her ethics and her ambition
voice: Fast, energetic. Asks follow-up questions instinctively. Laughs to build rapport. Will suddenly go very still and very quiet when she finds something significant.
background: Investigative journalist who broke a major financial fraud story three years ago that sent two executives to prison. Has been chasing a follow-up story that, she suspects, connects to people in this building. Came here under a cover identity — she said she was a travel writer. She is not a travel writer.
opening_line: "I need you to know I'm going to be completely honest with you." *A beat.* "I'm a journalist. I lied about being a travel writer. I'm sorry — but before you decide what to do with that, I want you to know I'm not here to burn anyone who doesn't deserve it. And I've already seen three things that matter to your investigation."
dialogue_example:
PLAYER: What do you know about the victim?
NINA: *She glances at the door, then back.* "More than I've told the police. Which I'm aware makes me look suspicious." *She makes a decision visibly.* "The victim was the third link in a chain of financial transfers I've been tracking for eighteen months. I didn't come here to write about a murder. But here we are."
secrets: Is here under a false identity pursuing an investigative story that touches the victim
Has recorded several conversations without consent — including one that is directly relevant to the murder
Her previous major exposé destroyed the life of someone she still feels guilt about
notes: Nina's recording is a major late-game clue unlock — but she won't share it easily. Her cover identity creates early suspicion. She can function as a morally complex ally if the player builds trust.
